rationale: Three concrete shifts in Senior AI Infrastructure Engineer postings observed during 2026-03-01 to 2026-06-01 drive these additive proposals. (1) LLM-as-judge has crossed from research curiosity to production CI gate: postings from Reddit, Airbnb, Zoom, GE Vernova, and Affinity now ask for evaluation pipelines, calibration vs human labels, and regression-on-merge — none of which is covered in the current mod-206 syllabus that stops at MLflow/drift detection. (2) Agentic systems infrastructure has matured into a distinct line item with Temporal, multi-agent coordination, and AIOps-style auto-remediation appearing as senior-level requirements at Scale AI Enterprise (2026-06-01), Justworks (2026-06-01), Synthesia (2026-05), Affinity (2026-06-01), Airbnb (2026-05), and Recursion (2026-05) — current curriculum has no module that owns the operational concerns of agents in production (durable workflows, state management, tool invocation reliability, observability over agent traces). (3) HPC/Slurm has not gone away — postings from Wex (2026-02-12), Centific (2026-05), Perplexity (2026-04-07), and Cedana (2026-04) all explicitly require Slurm administration and Slurm/K8s bridging (Kueue, multi-cluster Slurm federation); mod-201 covers gang scheduling and K8s-native batch but does not address Slurm interop, which is now table-stakes for any AI infra role that touches large training clusters. All three additions are strictly additive, target clear gaps the senior level genuinely owns, and each is backed by 3+ distinct in-window postings. Items found in postings but already covered (vLLM/KV cache in mod-204, MIG/DCGM in mod-203, ArgoCD in mod-208, multi-cloud IaC in mod-205/208) are NOT proposed. Items that belong at a different level per the ownership rule (LLM/AI security → ai-infra-security-learning; HPC networking depth → ai-infra-performance-learning; vector DBs and prompt engineering → ai-infra-engineer-learning module 10) are NOT proposed here; JOB_REQUIREMENTS.md links to those owners instead.

modules.title: Agentic Systems Infrastructure in Production
modules.description: Build and operate the infrastructure that runs LLM agents in production: durable workflow engines (Temporal), multi-agent coordination patterns, agent state management, tool invocation reliability, observability over agent traces, and AIOps-style use of agents to reduce infra toil. Covers failure modes specific to agentic systems (infinite loops, tool-call thrash, cost explosions, prompt-injected control flow) and how to instrument and bound them with the same SRE discipline already covered for traditional services in mod-207. Estimated 35 hours: 12h lectures + 18h labs + 5h review. Prerequisites: mod-206 (Advanced MLOps), mod-207 (Observability & SRE), and basic familiarity with LLM application patterns from the engineer-level LLM Infrastructure module.
modules.rationale: Six in-window senior-level postings (Scale AI Enterprise 2026-06-01, Justworks 2026-06-01, Synthesia 2026-05, Affinity 2026-06-01, Airbnb 2026-05, Recursion 2026-05) explicitly require building agentic AI infrastructure in production. The requirements span durable workflow engines (Temporal explicitly named), multi-agent coordination, agent observability, and agentic remediation of ops toil — none of these is covered by any existing module 201-210. The peer-level ML Platform and MLOps tracks own model-side concerns (fine-tuning, registries) but do not own the production runtime/orchestration concerns for agent systems, which are a Senior AI Infrastructure Engineer responsibility. Covers requirements req-agentic-workflow-infra, req-agentic-systems-ops-automation, and req-temporal-workflow-orchestration from job-requirements.json.

exercise.title: LLM Evaluation Infrastructure as a CI/CD Gate
exercise.summary: Build a production-grade LLM evaluation pipeline that gates model and prompt deployments. Implement LLM-as-judge with calibration against a human-labeled golden set, RAGAS-style RAG quality metrics (faithfulness, context precision/recall, answer relevance), regression-on-merge that blocks promotion when a new prompt or model degrades evals beyond a threshold, and nightly regression sweeps with alert routing. Integrate with the existing mod-206 MLflow registry and mod-208 GitOps pipeline so that eval verdicts become a first-class promotion gate alongside accuracy and drift checks. Compare scoring with a frontier judge model vs a smaller open-weights judge; report calibration drift over time.

exercise.title: HPC ↔ Kubernetes: Slurm Administration, Kueue, and the Bridge
exercise.summary: Stand up a small Slurm cluster (3 nodes) with GPU resources via gres, configure fairshare and a QOS policy, then layer Kueue (the CNCF Kubernetes-native batch queueing project) on a parallel K8s cluster. Implement a Slurm/K8s bridge that lets a single job submission system route training jobs to whichever backend has capacity, with consistent quotas across both. Cover prolog/epilog hooks for GPU health checking, gang scheduling parity between Slurm srun and Kueue ClusterQueues, and a single Prometheus dashboard that surfaces job state across both backends. The exercise should make the student fluent in why large AI labs still run Slurm alongside K8s and how to make the two cooperate.
